title: switch expression
instruction:
Modern switch expression returns a value. No break needed.

Run this.
successMessage: Relational patterns (< 0, 0) and the discard _ default. Switch expressions are exhaustive — the compiler warns about missing cases.

title: Type patterns
instruction:
Match on the runtime type of an object.

Run this.
successMessage: Type patterns bind the matched value to a variable (n, s). when adds an extra condition. The order matters — more specific patterns first.

title: Property patterns
instruction:
Match specific property values with property patterns.

Run this.
successMessage: Property patterns { X: > 0, Y: > 0 } check multiple properties simultaneously. or combines patterns.

title: Tuple patterns
instruction:
Match on tuples — great for combining conditions.

Run this.
successMessage: Tuple patterns match on multiple values simultaneously. var x in a pattern captures the value.

title: Deconstruction
instruction:
Deconstruct objects into variables with tuple-style assignment.

Run this.
successMessage: Deconstruction extracts fields into separate variables. Records support deconstruction automatically.

title: List patterns (C# 11)
instruction:
Match on the contents of a list or array.

Run this.
successMessage: List patterns match array/list contents. .. is the 'rest' pattern. This is C# 11 — modern and expressive.

title: Combining patterns with and/or/not
successMessage: and/or/not combine patterns inline. >= 'a' and <= 'z' is a range check — cleaner than two separate conditions.

title: Pattern matching in real code
instruction:
Pattern matching shines in domain logic.

Run this.
successMessage: Records support positional deconstruction in patterns. The switch is exhaustive and type-safe. Adding a new Shape type requires updating Area — the compiler helps you find all the places.
